intro: Cleome bahiensis, also known as Bahian spider flower, is a flowering plant native to Brazil, belonging to the Cleomaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome bahiensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Cleome bahiensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and fever, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
